heroPoems.title: Sparks Upward
heroPoems.firstStanza:
A person is born for trouble
as surely
as sparks fly upward.

heroPoems.title: No Lost Effort
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Nothing sincere is wasted.
Practice deposits light
across lifetimes.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus II, 12
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Will transformation not be your work?
Earth, beloved, I will.
Believe me,
you no longer need your springtimes
to win me over.
One, ah one, is already too much for my blood.

heroPoems.title: No hope bears fruit, no shape of relief appears.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
No hope bears fruit, no shape of relief appears.
Why does night keep returning to my house?

heroPoems.title: Mr. Eliot's Sunday Morning Service
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Look, look, master, here comes two religious
caterpillars.
The Jew of Malta.

heroPoems.title: When thoughts run out
heroPoems.firstStanza:
When thoughts run out
I step into the woods
and gather
shepherd's purse.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: If thou speakest not I will fill my
heroPoems.firstStanza:
If thou speakest not I will fill my heart with thy silence and endure
it. I will keep still and wait like the night with starry vigil and its
head bent low with patience.
The morning will surely come, the darkness will vanish, and thy voice
pour down in golden streams breaking through the sky.
Then thy words will take wing in songs from every one of my birds’

heroPoems.title: To Autumn
A Boundless Moment
heroPoems.firstStanza:
He halted in the wind, and—what was that
Far in the maples, pale, but not a ghost?
He stood there bringing March against his thought,
And yet too ready to believe the most.
“Oh, that’s the Paradise-in-bloom,” I said;
And truly it was fair enough for flowers
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Holy Sonnet 10
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Death, be not proud, though some have called thee
Mighty and dreadful, for thou art not so,
For those whom thou think'st thou dost overthrow
Die not, poor Death, nor yet canst thou kill me.
From rest and sleep, which but thy pictures be,
Much pleasure; then from thee much more must flow,

collections.name: The Space Beside Your Name
collections.tagline: For the minute before the call.
collections.theme: Poems that make waiting feel textured rather than empty.

featuredPoem.title: Open your eyes of love, and see Him who pervades this world I
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Open your eyes of love, and see Him who pervades this world I
consider it well, and know that this is your own country.

poems.title: Faith is not a blanket, it is a fire alarm.
When He Himself reveals Himself, Brahma brings into manifestation

collections.name: At the Edge of Warm Tea
collections.tagline: For when clocks feel unusually loud.
collections.theme: A muted field of recollection, where detail carries the emotional weight.

featuredPoem.title: Endurance Doctrine
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Sensation is weather.
Heat, cold, pleasure, pain—

poems.title: Spring is here and the rose has risen from dust
You Darkness, from Which I Come

collections.name: The Harbor of Unfinished Lines
collections.tagline: For when home feels slightly distant.
collections.theme: Poems that trace the distance between nearness and speech.

featuredPoem.title: Do not throw blame at the drunkard's door
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Do not throw blame at the drunkard's door
keep your own page clean before you mark mine

poems.title: This heart was never made for obedience.
I watch people in this world
